positives_detail: Zoned support cushions joints, provides proper spinal alignment, and reinforced edges.
A queen size can often be found for under $1,000 before discounts, so we think the Silk & Snow is a natural choice for anyone with a strict budget.
It has a medium soft feel that is plush and adaptive on the surface, offering strong support with plenty of bounce in firmer options.
Individually pocketed coils minimize motion transfer, perfect for couples and restless sleepers.
Has a button-tufted cover design.
Contains minimal advanced cooling features.
Features an antimicrobial cover to maintain a cleaner sleep environment.
Our pressure map showed that the Silk & Snow Hybrid puts hardly any pressure on the back. So it could be a sensible choice for anyone who experiences back pain.
Constructed with soybean-oil memory foam.
Its comfort system combines memory foam layers with a zoned pocketed coil support core and foam encasement for balanced cushioning and support.
We measured its temperature increase with our thermal gun and found it to have good cooling capabilities.
The mattress is infused with cooling gel memory foam, ideal for those who tend to sleep hot.
The Silk & Snow Hybrid is ideal for side sleepers and most back and stomach sleepers.
negatives_detail: Needs a few days to air out due to off-gassing before optimal use.
